dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses two moving averages of different speeds: faster (ema_10) for timing/surge detection, slower (ema_50) for primary trend direction. Entry occurs when faster MA crosses and aligns with slower MA trend. Exit on reversal of faster MA. ema_20 used as intermediate trend confirmation. Parameters left as provided by allowed indicators since author did not specify exact periods—chose common Kaufman-aligned periods (10/20/50).

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses two moving averages of different speeds: faster (ema_10) for timing/surge detection, slower (ema_50) for primary trend direction. Entry occurs when faster MA crosses and aligns with slower MA trend. Exit on reversal of faster MA. ema_20 used as intermediate trend confirmation. Parameters left as provided by allowed indicators since author did not specify exact periods—chose common Kaufman-aligned periods (10/20/50).

source_rule_text: Enter a trade when there is a recent short-term surge of prices in the direction of the major trend, identified by using a faster moving average for timing aligned with a slower moving average for the primary trend direction.
